title: Simulation and Game Analysis
text: Larger runs (thousands games) expose real first-move structure. Pure-R self-play intentionally throughput ceiling release; Rcpp acceleration future enhancement.
code: sim <- tsr_simulate("random", "random", n_games = 10L, seed = 1L, verbose = FALSE) sim #> # A tibble: 10 × 10 #>    game_id policy_x policy_o winner n_moves first_move first_move_i first_move_j #>      <int> <chr>    <chr>     <int>   <int>      <int>        <int>        <int> #>  1       1 random   random        2     100        249            0            2 #>  2       2 random   random        1      81        107            2            2 #>  3       3 random   random        2      98        174            1            3 #>  4       4 random   random        1      55         31            2            3 #>  5       5 random   random        1     141        103            2            1 #>  6       6 random   random        2      82         47            2            3 #>  7       7 random   random        2      44         87            2            1 #>  8       8 random   random        1      85        115            2            0 #>  9       9 random   random        1      85        112            3            3 #> 10      10 random   random        1     129        211            2            0 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: first_move_k <int>, first_move_l <int> os <- tsr_opening_stats(sim, by = "first_move") head(os, 5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 7 #>   first_move n_games win_rate_x win_rate_o draw_rate ci_lo ci_hi #>        <int>   <int>      <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1         31       1          1          0         0 0.207 1     #> 2         47       1          0          1         0 0     0.793 #> 3         87       1          0          1         0 0     0.793 #> 4        103       1          1          0         0 0.207 1     #> 5        107       1          1          0         0 0.207 1

title: Simulation and Game Analysis
code: game <- tsr_play_game("greedy", "random", seed = 7L) analysis <- tsr_analyze_game(game) tail(analysis, 6) #> # A tibble: 6 × 17 #>     ply player  cell     i     j     k     l win_prob_before win_prob_after #>   <int>  <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <int>           <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1    14      2    22     1     1     1     0         0.0227         0.0258  #> 2    15      1    16     3     3     0     0         0.974          0.991   #> 3    16      2   233     0     2     2     3         0.00915        0.00964 #> 4    17      1    14     1     3     0     0         0.990          0.998   #> 5    18      2   130     1     0     0     2         0.00190        0.00200 #> 6    19      1     6     1     1     0     0         0.998          1       #> # ℹ 8 more variables: delta <dbl>, best_cell <int>, best_delta <dbl>, #> #   regret <dbl>, is_best <lgl>, missed_win <lgl>, missed_block <lgl>, #> #   is_turning_point <lgl> tsr_plot_winprob(analysis) tsr_turning_points(analysis) #> # A tibble: 1 × 17 #>     ply player  cell     i     j     k     l win_prob_before win_prob_after #>   <int>  <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <int>           <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1     5      1     2     1     0     0     0           0.548          0.740 #> # ℹ 8 more variables: delta <dbl>, best_cell <int>, best_delta <dbl>, #> #   regret <dbl>, is_best <lgl>, missed_win <lgl>, missed_block <lgl>, #> #   is_turning_point <lgl> tsr_game_summary(game) #> # A tibble: 1 × 10 #>   winner n_moves n_missed_wins_x n_missed_wins_o n_missed_blocks_x #>    <int>   <int>           <int>           <int>             <int> #> 1      1      19               6               0                 0 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: n_missed_blocks_o <int>, mean_regret_x <dbl>, #> #   mean_regret_o <dbl>, n_turning_points <int>, decisiveness <dbl>

title: Simulation and Game Analysis
code: games_r <- lapply(1:4, function(s) tsr_play_game("random", "random", seed = s)) games_g <- lapply(1:4, function(s) tsr_play_game("greedy", "random", seed = s)) p_random <- tsr_behavior_profile(games_r, side = 1L, label = "X random") p_greedy <- tsr_behavior_profile(games_g, side = 1L, label = "X greedy") tsr_compare_profiles(p_random, p_greedy) #> # A tibble: 2 × 11 #>   label     side n_games n_moves_total accuracy mean_regret blunder_rate #>   <chr>    <int>   <int>         <int>    <dbl>       <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 X random     1       4           177    0          0.376        0.870  #> 2 X greedy     1       4            96    0.125      0.0197       0.0833 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: win_conversion <dbl>, defense_rate <dbl>, #> #   aggression <dbl>, mean_decisiveness <dbl>

title: Getting Started with tesseractR
text: tesseractR plays tic-tac-toe four-dimensional 4×4×4×4 hypercube — 256 cells indexed (, j, k, l) coordinate 0:3. line four cells whose successive differences equal constant non-zero direction vector {−1, 0, 1}^4. deduplicating reversed directions 520 winning lines standard ((n+2)^d − n^d) / 2 formula d = n = 4.

title: Getting Started with tesseractR
code: b <- tsr_new_board() b <- tsr_move(b, 0L, 0L, 0L, 0L)   # X at (0,0,0,0) = cell 1 b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 17L)       # O b <- tsr_move(b, 1L, 0L, 0L, 0L)   # X at (1,0,0,0) = cell 2 print(b) #>  #> ── <ttt_board> ── #>  #> 4x4x4x4 board, 256 cells #> moves played: 3 #> to move: O #> legal moves: 253 tsr_plot(b)

title: Getting Started with tesseractR
text: continue game X completes axis-aligned line:
code: b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 18L)       # O b <- tsr_move(b, 2L, 0L, 0L, 0L)   # X b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 19L)       # O b <- tsr_move(b, 3L, 0L, 0L, 0L)   # X completes 1-2-3-4 tsr_check_win(b) #> [1] 1 tsr_winning_line(b) #> [1] 1 2 3 4 tsr_plot_slice(b, axis = "l", at = 0L)

title: Getting Started with tesseractR
text: tsr_ai_move() returns chosen linear cell index. Difficulty 1 always takes immediate win blocks immediate threat. Higher difficulties look ahead (search exponential pure R — depth 4 may take several seconds).
code: b <- tsr_new_board() b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 1L)        # human X ai_cell <- tsr_ai_move(b, difficulty = 1L) b <- tsr_move(b, cell = ai_cell)   # AI O ai_cell #> [1] 4

title: Getting Started with tesseractR
code: b <- tsr_new_board() b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 1L); b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 17L) b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 2L) tsr_win_prob(b, player = 1L) #> [1] 0.5502794 head(tsr_rate_moves(b)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>    cell     i     j     k     l score win_prob  rank is_best is_winning #>   <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <dbl>    <dbl> <int> <lgl>   <lgl>      #> 1     4     3     0     0     0     1    0.502     1 TRUE    FALSE      #> 2    86     1     1     1     1     0    0.5       2 FALSE   FALSE      #> 3   155     2     2     1     2     0    0.5       3 FALSE   FALSE      #> 4     3     2     0     0     0    -6    0.487     4 FALSE   FALSE      #> 5    20     3     0     1     0    -7    0.485     5 FALSE   FALSE      #> 6    22     1     1     1     0    -7    0.485     6 FALSE   FALSE      #> # ℹ 1 more variable: is_blocking <lgl>

title: Getting Started with tesseractR
text: tsr_run_app() launches interactive Shiny version live move-rating overlay game-analysis panel. See “Simulation Game Analysis” vignette self-play analytics.

what: Four-Dimensional Tic-Tac-Toe with a Heuristic AI Opponent
title: Four-Dimensional Tic-Tac-Toe with a Heuristic AI Opponent
text: tesseractR plays four-dimensional tic-tac-toe 4×4×4×4 hypercube (256 cells, 520 winning lines), depth-limited negamax AI, ggplot2 visualization hypercube 4×4 grid 4×4 boards, interactive Shiny app live move evaluation game-analysis panel.

title: Four-Dimensional Tic-Tac-Toe with a Heuristic AI Opponent
Four-Dimensional Tic-Tac-Toe with a Heuristic AI Opponent
text: Launch interactive app tsr_run_app() (requires shiny).
code: library(tesseractR) b <- tsr_new_board() b <- tsr_move(b, 0L, 0L, 0L, 0L)   # X at (0,0,0,0) b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 17L)       # O b <- tsr_move(b, 1L, 0L, 0L, 0L)   # X print(b) #>  #> ── <ttt_board> ── #>  #> 4x4x4x4 board, 256 cells #> moves played: 3 #> to move: O #> legal moves: 253 tsr_plot(b)

what: tesseractR: Four-Dimensional Tic-Tac-Toe with a Heuristic AI Opponent — tesseractR-package
title: tesseractR: Four-Dimensional Tic-Tac-Toe with a Heuristic AI Opponent — tesseractR-package
text: Play four-dimensional tic-tac-toe four--four--four--four hypercube, first player align four marks along straight line four-dimensional space wins. Provides game engine legal-move generation win detection across 520 winning lines, depth-limited negamax artificial-intelligence opponent adjustable difficulty, 'ggplot2' visualization hypercube grid boards, interactive 'shiny' application play.

title: tesseractR: Four-Dimensional Tic-Tac-Toe with a Heuristic AI Opponent — tesseractR-package
text: Maintainer: Raban Heller raban.heller@charite.de (ORCID) Authors: Raban Heller raban.heller@charite.de (ORCID)

what: AI move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_ai_move
title: AI move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_ai_move
text: Returns linear cell index AI's chosen move using depth-limited negamax alpha-beta pruning. difficulty >= 1, immediate winning move always taken immediate opponent threat always blocked.

title: AI move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_ai_move
AI move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_ai_move
text: board ttt_board. difficulty Integer 1:4 (default 2L). Maps search depth.

title: AI move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_ai_move
text: Integer scalar: chosen cell's linear index 1:256.

title: AI move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_ai_move
text: Tie-breaking among moves equal evaluation deterministic (lowest linear index wins). Note: search exponential depth runs pure R; depth 4 may take several seconds 256-cell board. Rcpp acceleration planned future enhancement intentionally scope release.

title: AI move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_ai_move

what: Analyze a played game move by move — tsr_analyze_game
title: Analyze a played game move by move — tsr_analyze_game
text: Walks tsr_game (tsr_play_game()) computes per-ply evaluation context using S3 evaluation core: win probability /move, move's delta, best alternative move, regret, flags missed wins / missed blocks / turning points.

title: Analyze a played game move by move — tsr_analyze_game
Analyze a played game move by move — tsr_analyze_game
text: game tsr_game object. method One "heuristic" (default) "rollout". Passed underlying tsr_win_prob() / tsr_rate_moves(). n Integer. Rollouts method = "rollout".

title: Analyze a played game move by move — tsr_analyze_game
text: tibble, one row per ply, columns: ply, player, cell, , j, k, l, win_prob_before, win_prob_after, delta, best_cell, best_delta, regret, is_best, missed_win, missed_block, is_turning_point.

title: Analyze a played game move by move — tsr_analyze_game
text: turning point ply abs(delta) >= 0.15.

title: Analyze a played game move by move — tsr_analyze_game
code: # \donttest{ g <- tsr_play_game("random", "random", seed = 1L) tsr_analyze_game(g) #> # A tibble: 100 × 17 #>      ply player  cell     i     j     k     l win_prob_before win_prob_after #>    <int>  <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <int>           <dbl>          <dbl> #>  1     1      1   249     0     2     3     3           0.5            0.518 #>  2     2      2    68     3     0     0     1           0.482          0.5   #>  3     3      1   168     3     1     2     2           0.5            0.518 #>  4     4      2   130     1     0     0     2           0.482          0.496 #>  5     5      1   164     3     0     2     2           0.504          0.535 #>  6     6      2   219     2     2     1     3           0.465          0.482 #>  7     7      1    43     2     2     2     0           0.518          0.535 #>  8     8      2    14     1     3     0     0           0.465          0.482 #>  9     9      1   216     3     1     1     3           0.518          0.531 #> 10    10      2   193     0     0     0     3           0.469          0.520 #> # ℹ 90 more rows #> # ℹ 8 more variables: delta <dbl>, best_cell <int>, best_delta <dbl>, #> #   regret <dbl>, is_best <lgl>, missed_win <lgl>, missed_block <lgl>, #> #   is_turning_point <lgl> # }

what: Aggregate behavioral profile for a side across many games — tsr_behavior_profile
title: Aggregate behavioral profile for a side across many games — tsr_behavior_profile
text: Walks list tsr_game objects, analyses , computes aggregate profile chosen side (player X default).

title: Aggregate behavioral profile for a side across many games — tsr_behavior_profile
Aggregate behavioral profile for a side across many games — tsr_behavior_profile
text: games list tsr_game objects. side Integer 1L (X) 2L (O). side profile. label Optional character. Label profile (e.g. policy name).

title: Aggregate behavioral profile for a side across many games — tsr_behavior_profile
text: one-row tibble label, side, n_games, n_moves_total, accuracy, mean_regret, blunder_rate, win_conversion, defense_rate, aggression, mean_decisiveness.

title: Aggregate behavioral profile for a side across many games — tsr_behavior_profile
code: # \donttest{ g1 <- tsr_play_game("random", "random", seed = 1L) g2 <- tsr_play_game("random", "random", seed = 2L) tsr_behavior_profile(list(g1, g2), side = 1L, label = "random") #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>   label   side n_games n_moves_total accuracy mean_regret blunder_rate #>   <chr>  <int>   <int>         <int>    <dbl>       <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 random     1       2            95        0       0.410        0.842 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: win_conversion <dbl>, defense_rate <dbl>, #> #   aggression <dbl>, mean_decisiveness <dbl> # }

title: Detect a winner on the board — tsr_check_win
text: Scans cached win-line table returns winning player's mark 0L player completed line.

Detect a winner on the board — tsr_check_win
text: board ttt_board.

title: Detect a winner on the board — tsr_check_win
text: Integer scalar: 0L (winner), 1L (player X), 2L (player O).

title: Detect a winner on the board — tsr_check_win
Combine multiple behavioral profiles — tsr_compare_profiles
text: Row-binds sequence tsr_behavior_profile() tibbles side--side comparison. Errors mismatched columns.

title: Combine multiple behavioral profiles — tsr_compare_profiles
Combine multiple behavioral profiles — tsr_compare_profiles
text: ... Tibbles produced tsr_behavior_profile().

title: Combine multiple behavioral profiles — tsr_compare_profiles
text: tibble one row per input.

title: Combine multiple behavioral profiles — tsr_compare_profiles
code: # \donttest{ g1 <- tsr_play_game("random", "random", seed = 1L) p1 <- tsr_behavior_profile(list(g1), side = 1L, label = "random") p2 <- tsr_behavior_profile(list(g1), side = 2L, label = "random") tsr_compare_profiles(p1, p2) #> # A tibble: 2 × 11 #>   label   side n_games n_moves_total accuracy mean_regret blunder_rate #>   <chr>  <int>   <int>         <int>    <dbl>       <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 random     1       1            50        0       0.512         0.94 #> 2 random     2       1            50        0       0.147         0.6  #> # ℹ 4 more variables: win_conversion <dbl>, defense_rate <dbl>, #> #   aggression <dbl>, mean_decisiveness <dbl> # }

title: Evaluate a position — tsr_evaluate
text: Heuristic positional score perspective player. Positive favors player. scale unitless comparable within board size; raw input fed calibrated win-probability mapping.

title: Evaluate a position — tsr_evaluate
Evaluate a position — tsr_evaluate
text: board ttt_board. player Integer 1L 2L. NULL (default), position evaluated board$to_move's perspective.

title: Evaluate a position — tsr_evaluate
text: Numeric scalar.

title: Evaluate a position — tsr_evaluate
text: Terminal positions short-circuit: win returns large positive sentinel (approximately 1e6), loss negation, draw 0.

title: Evaluate a position — tsr_evaluate

what: One-row summary for a played game — tsr_game_summary
title: One-row summary for a played game — tsr_game_summary
text: Compact scorecard missed-win / missed-block counts, mean regret per player, turning-point count, final decisiveness.

title: One-row summary for a played game — tsr_game_summary
One-row summary for a played game — tsr_game_summary
text: game tsr_game.

title: One-row summary for a played game — tsr_game_summary
text: one-row tibble columns: winner, n_moves, n_missed_wins_x, n_missed_wins_o, n_missed_blocks_x, n_missed_blocks_o, mean_regret_x, mean_regret_o, n_turning_points, decisiveness.

title: One-row summary for a played game — tsr_game_summary
code: # \donttest{ g <- tsr_play_game("random", "random", seed = 1L) tsr_game_summary(g) #> # A tibble: 1 × 10 #>   winner n_moves n_missed_wins_x n_missed_wins_o n_missed_blocks_x #>    <int>   <int>           <int>           <int>             <int> #> 1      2     100              25              29                 5 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: n_missed_blocks_o <int>, mean_regret_x <dbl>, #> #   mean_regret_o <dbl>, n_turning_points <int>, decisiveness <dbl> # }

what: Make a move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_move
title: Make a move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_move
text: Place current player's mark given cell return new ttt_board move applied. input board never mutated.

title: Make a move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_move
code: tsr_move(board, i = NULL, j = NULL, k = NULL, l = NULL, cell = NULL)

title: Make a move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_move
text: board ttt_board. , j, k, l Integer coordinates 0:3. Either four none. cell Optional linear cell index (1:256). Mutually exclusive coordinate form.

title: Make a move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_move
text: new ttt_board mark placed, to_move flipped, history extended move.

title: Make a move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_move
text: Provide either four hypercube coordinates (, j, k, l) (0:3), linear cell index via cell (integer 1:256). Exactly one form must supplied.

title: Make a move on a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_move
code: b <- tsr_new_board() b <- tsr_move(b, 0L, 0L, 0L, 0L) b <- tsr_move(b, cell = 5L) tsr_status(b) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   winner is_full is_over n_moves to_move n_legal #>    <int> <lgl>   <lgl>     <int>   <int>   <int> #> 1      0 FALSE   FALSE         2       1     254

what: Create a new, empty 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_new_board
title: Create a new, empty 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_new_board
text: Constructs fresh ttt_board representing empty 4x4x4x4 hypercube player X (mark 1) move.

title: Create a new, empty 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_new_board
Create a new, empty 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_new_board
text: object class ttt_board: list components state (integer vector length 256, values 0:2), to_move (1L X, 2L O), history (integer vector linear cell indices played, order).

title: Create a new, empty 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_new_board
code: b <- tsr_new_board() is_ttt_board(b) #> [1] TRUE tsr_status(b) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   winner is_full is_over n_moves to_move n_legal #>    <int> <lgl>   <lgl>     <int>   <int>   <int> #> 1      0 FALSE   FALSE         0       1     256

what: Opening statistics from a simulation — tsr_opening_stats
title: Opening statistics from a simulation — tsr_opening_stats
text: Aggregates tsr_simulate() result per-group win rates Wilson confidence intervals.

title: Opening statistics from a simulation — tsr_opening_stats
Opening statistics from a simulation — tsr_opening_stats
text: sim tibble produced tsr_simulate(). Character. Grouping column(s). Defaults "first_move".

title: Opening statistics from a simulation — tsr_opening_stats
text: tibble columns: grouping column(s), n_games, win_rate_x, win_rate_o, draw_rate, ci_lo, ci_hi (Wilson 95% CI win_rate_x).

title: Opening statistics from a simulation — tsr_opening_stats
code: # \donttest{ sim <- tsr_simulate("random", "random", n_games = 5L, seed = 1L, verbose = FALSE) tsr_opening_stats(sim) #> # A tibble: 5 × 7 #>   first_move n_games win_rate_x win_rate_o draw_rate ci_lo ci_hi #>        <int>   <int>      <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1         31       1          1          0         0 0.207 1     #> 2        103       1          1          0         0 0.207 1     #> 3        107       1          1          0         0 0.207 1     #> 4        174       1          0          1         0 0     0.793 #> 5        249       1          0          1         0 0     0.793 # }

what: Play a single game between two policies — tsr_play_game
title: Play a single game between two policies — tsr_play_game
text: Simulates one complete 4D tic-tac-toe game given policies player X player O. Returns tsr_game object recording move sequence outcome.

title: Play a single game between two policies — tsr_play_game
Play a single game between two policies — tsr_play_game
text: policy_x Policy player X. Either built-string function (board) -> integer cell index. policy_o Policy player O. forms policy_x. seed Optional integer. supplied, RNG seeded via withr::local_seed(); global .Random.seed unchanged.

title: Play a single game between two policies — tsr_play_game
text: tsr_game object: list moves (integer vector cell indices, order), winner (0/1/2), n_moves (integer), policies (named character c(x, o)), final_board (ttt_board), to_move (integer vector — moving player ply).

title: Play a single game between two policies — tsr_play_game
text: Built-policy strings: "random", "greedy", "ai" (depth 2), "aiN" N 1:4 (e.g. "ai3").

title: Play a single game between two policies — tsr_play_game

title: Plot a 4D tic-tac-toe board as a 4x4 grid of 4x4 boards — tsr_plot
text: Renders 256-cell hypercube 4x4 outer grid (indexed (k, l)), outer cell contains 4x4 inner board (indexed (, j)). Returns ggplot object; never renders place.

Plot a 4D tic-tac-toe board as a 4x4 grid of 4x4 boards — tsr_plot

what: Plot a 3D slice of a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_plot_slice
title: Plot a 3D slice of a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_plot_slice
text: Holds one four hypercube axes fixed value, dropping board 3D 4x4x4 cube, renders cube 1x4 strip 4x4 mini-boards. Use view make --four-dimensions diagonals read straight lines within single plane. Returns ggplot object.

title: Plot a 3D slice of a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_plot_slice
code: tsr_plot_slice(   board,   axis = c("i", "j", "k", "l"),   at = 0L,   highlight_win = TRUE,   ... )

title: Plot a 3D slice of a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_plot_slice
text: board ttt_board. axis One "", "j", "k", "l". axis hold fixed. Integer 0:3. value fix axis. highlight_win Logical. Emphasise winning line lies (intersects) slice. ... Reserved future arguments.

title: Plot a 3D slice of a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_plot_slice
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot a 3D slice of a 4D tic-tac-toe board — tsr_plot_slice
text: Convention: three remaining axes, highest-numbered used block axis (strip dimension); two form mini-board.

what: Rate every legal move from a position — tsr_rate_moves
title: Rate every legal move from a position — tsr_rate_moves
text: legal move, applies scores resulting position moving side's perspective. Returns tibble sorted best-first type- stable columns; finished board returns zero-row tibble schema.

title: Rate every legal move from a position — tsr_rate_moves
Rate every legal move from a position — tsr_rate_moves
text: board ttt_board. method One "heuristic" (default) "rollout". n Integer. Number rollouts method = "rollout".

title: Rate every legal move from a position — tsr_rate_moves
text: tibble columns: cell (integer linear index), , j, k, l (integer coordinates 0:3), score (numeric raw evaluation move), win_prob (numeric [0, 1]), rank (integer; 1 = best), is_best (logical), is_winning (logical; move completes line), is_blocking (logical; move denies opponent's immediate win). Sorted rank.

title: Rate every legal move from a position — tsr_rate_moves
code: tsr_rate_moves(tsr_new_board()) #> # A tibble: 256 × 11 #>     cell     i     j     k     l score win_prob  rank is_best is_winning #>    <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> <dbl>    <dbl> <int> <lgl>   <lgl>      #>  1     1     0     0     0     0    15    0.533     1 TRUE    FALSE      #>  2     4     3     0     0     0    15    0.533     2 FALSE   FALSE      #>  3    13     0     3     0     0    15    0.533     3 FALSE   FALSE      #>  4    16     3     3     0     0    15    0.533     4 FALSE   FALSE      #>  5    49     0     0     3     0    15    0.533     5 FALSE   FALSE      #>  6    52     3     0     3     0    15    0.533     6 FALSE   FALSE      #>  7    61     0     3     3     0    15    0.533     7 FALSE   FALSE      #>  8    64     3     3     3     0    15    0.533     8 FALSE   FALSE      #>  9    86     1     1     1     1    15    0.533     9 FALSE   FALSE      #> 10    87     2     1     1     1    15    0.533    10 FALSE   FALSE      #> # ℹ 246 more rows #> # ℹ 1 more variable: is_blocking <lgl>

what: Simulate many games between two policies — tsr_simulate
title: Simulate many games between two policies — tsr_simulate
text: Runs n_games self-play games returns tibble summarising game.

title: Simulate many games between two policies — tsr_simulate
code: tsr_simulate(policy_x, policy_o, n_games = 100L, seed = NULL, verbose = TRUE)

title: Simulate many games between two policies — tsr_simulate
text: policy_x Policy player X. See tsr_play_game(). policy_o Policy player O. n_games Integer. Number games play (default 100L). seed Optional integer. Seeds RNG via withr::local_seed(); global .Random.seed unchanged. verbose Logical. Show progress bar interactive sessions.

title: Simulate many games between two policies — tsr_simulate
text: tibble, one row per game, columns: game_id (integer), policy_x (character), policy_o (character), winner (integer 0/1/2), n_moves (integer), first_move (integer cell), first_move_i, first_move_j, first_move_k, first_move_l (integer).

title: Simulate many games between two policies — tsr_simulate
text: Performance: pure-R self-play 256 cells slow, deep-AI policies multiply cost. Meaningful opening statistics may need thousands games; hot-path functions flagged future Rcpp replacement.

title: Simulate many games between two policies — tsr_simulate
code: # \donttest{ tsr_simulate("random", "random", n_games = 5L, seed = 1L, verbose = FALSE) #> # A tibble: 5 × 10 #>   game_id policy_x policy_o winner n_moves first_move first_move_i first_move_j #>     <int> <chr>    <chr>     <int>   <int>      <int>        <int>        <int> #> 1       1 random   random        2     100        249            0            2 #> 2       2 random   random        1      81        107            2            2 #> 3       3 random   random        2      98        174            1            3 #> 4       4 random   random        1      55         31            2            3 #> 5       5 random   random        1     141        103            2            1 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: first_move_k <int>, first_move_l <int> # }

title: Structured one-row status for a board — tsr_status

what: Undo the most recent moves on a board — tsr_undo
title: Undo the most recent moves on a board — tsr_undo
text: Pops n moves end history, clearing cells restoring player--move. Returns new board.

title: Undo the most recent moves on a board — tsr_undo
Undo the most recent moves on a board — tsr_undo
text: board ttt_board. n Integer (default 1L). Number moves undo. Must exceed length(board$history).

title: Undo the most recent moves on a board — tsr_undo
text: new ttt_board moves removed.

title: Undo the most recent moves on a board — tsr_undo
code: b <- tsr_new_board() b2 <- tsr_move(b, cell = 1L) identical(tsr_undo(b2), b) #> [1] TRUE

what: Calibrated win probability for a position — tsr_win_prob
title: Calibrated win probability for a position — tsr_win_prob
text: Returns estimated probability [0, 1] player wins given position. Two methods supported:

title: Calibrated win probability for a position — tsr_win_prob
code: tsr_win_prob(   board,   player = NULL,   method = c("heuristic", "rollout"),   n = 200L )

title: Calibrated win probability for a position — tsr_win_prob
text: board ttt_board. player Integer 1L 2L, NULL (default, uses board$to_move). method One "heuristic" (default) "rollout". n Integer. Number rollouts method = "rollout".

title: Calibrated win probability for a position — tsr_win_prob
text: Numeric scalar [0, 1].

title: Calibrated win probability for a position — tsr_win_prob
text: "heuristic" (default, real-time): maps tsr_evaluate() logistic calibration p = plogis((score - ) / b). coefficients , b fit data-raw/ script (S6) stored internal package data; provisional defaults used calibration run. "rollout" (offline accuracy): runs n policy-guided Monte-Carlo playouts using light heuristic policy returns empirical win share. Slower pure R; intended offline analysis, live UI. Terminal positions short-circuit: win returns 1, loss 0, draw 0.5.

title: Calibrated win probability for a position — tsr_win_prob

what: Print methods for a 4D tic-tac-toe board — ttt_board-methods
title: Print methods for a 4D tic-tac-toe board — ttt_board-methods
text: S3 format, print, summary methods ttt_board. format() returns character vector summary, print() emits one-screen cli summary returns input invisibly, summary() returns tsr_status() tibble.

title: Print methods for a 4D tic-tac-toe board — ttt_board-methods
code: # S3 method for class 'ttt_board' format(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'ttt_board' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'ttt_board' summary(object, ...)

title: Print methods for a 4D tic-tac-toe board — ttt_board-methods
text: x, object ttt_board. ... Reserved.

title: Print methods for a 4D tic-tac-toe board — ttt_board-methods
text: format() returns character vector. print() returns x invisibly. summary() returns one-row tibble.

title: Print methods for a 4D tic-tac-toe board — ttt_board-methods
code: format(tsr_new_board()) #> [1] "<ttt_board>"              "4x4x4x4 board, 256 cells" #> [3] "moves played: 0"          "to move: X"               #> [5] "legal moves: 256"         summary(tsr_new_board()) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   winner is_full is_over n_moves to_move n_legal #>    <int> <lgl>   <lgl>     <int>   <int>   <int> #> 1      0 FALSE   FALSE         0       1     256

title: tesseractR 0.1.0
text: Game engine 4D tic-tac-toe 4x4x4x4 hypercube win detection across 520 winning lines (tsr_new_board(), tsr_move(), tsr_undo(), tsr_legal_moves(), tsr_check_win(), tsr_winning_line(), tsr_is_full(), tsr_status()). Depth-limited negamax AI opponent adjustable difficulty (tsr_ai_move()). Position evaluation calibrated win probability (tsr_evaluate(), tsr_win_prob()), plus per-move ratings identifying best next move (tsr_rate_moves()). Self-play simulation opening/strategy statistics (tsr_play_game(), tsr_simulate(), tsr_opening_stats()). Game analysis play-behavior analytics: move--move evaluation, turning points, missed wins/blocks, regret, behavioral profiling (tsr_analyze_game(), tsr_turning_points(), tsr_game_summary(), tsr_behavior_profile(), tsr_compare_profiles(), tsr_plot_winprob()). ggplot2 visualization hypercube grid boards (tsr_plot(), autoplot() method), plus 2D slice view fixing one axis (tsr_plot_slice()) 3D plotly cube view app. Interactive Shiny application real-time move evaluation game-analysis panel (tsr_run_app()).
